cramps.movement: Try lying on your back with legs up the wall, or a gentle child's pose to relieve pelvic pressure.
cramps.nutrition: Warm liquids like chamomile or raspberry leaf tea. Magnesium supplements or pumpkin seeds can reduce muscle spasms.

bloating.movement: Gentle twists (like seated spinal twists) can aid digestion. Avoid heavy core workouts.
bloating.nutrition: Stay extremely hydrated. Drink peppermint or dandelion root tea. Avoid excessive salt and highly processed carbs.

fatigue.movement: Scale back your planned workout. Focus on mobility and breathing. It's okay to just walk today.
fatigue.nutrition: Small, frequent meals with protein and complex carbs to avoid blood sugar crashes. Avoid heavy sugar.

headache.movement: Avoid jumping or inversions (keeping head below heart). Stick to steady, upright movements.
headache.nutrition: Hydrate immediately with electrolytes. Limit caffeine if it triggers you, or have a small amount if it helps.

low-mood.movement: Endorphins help! A brisk walk outside in the sun or your favorite upbeat dance workout.
low-mood.nutrition: Omega-3s (salmon, chia seeds) and dark chocolate can help boost serotonin.
